name: The Failures of Marginal Influence-Based Attribution Methods for Global Time Series Explanations

description: arXiv:2607.16236v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Explainability methods for time series models predominantly produce flat attribution scores: they quantify the direct influence of a feature at a timestamp by a scalar. We prove that the dominant failure mode of such methods is not the scalar format itself but a fundamental computational mismatch: existing methods compute scores via marginal con...
author: Amadeo Tunyi
